mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding employment income that feeds into aggregate demand and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS is rising year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0%, restrictive monetary policy increases discount rates and debt-service costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales and consumer prices signals overheating consumer demand that compresses real corporate margins and elevates monetary-policy risk, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and CPIAUCSL both rising month-over-month
failure_condition: Periods with jointly rising RSAFS and CPIAUCSL do not produce significantly negative 20-day S&P 500 returns relative to baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production after an industrial contraction signals manufacturing restocking and capacity utilization recovery, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises after declining for at least 6 months
failure_condition: During post-industrial-recession periods, rising INDPRO coincides with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts reflects construction-sector multiplier effects and household wealth growth that translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in HOUST are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 5.0% signals a tight labor market that triggers wage-push inflation expectations and anticipated Federal Reserve tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.0%, the S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of subsequent weeks.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 1.0% combined with accelerating retail sales validates a liquidity-fueled demand expansion that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint periods of FEDFUNDS below 1.0% and rising RSAFS fail to produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns (mean annualized return below 2%).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 3.0% annualized signals robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue acceleration and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth above 3.0% annualized are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of cases.
